subject: english
chapter_title: Yoga — A Way of Life (Sports and Wellness)

main_sections.title: Yoga — A Way of Life
main_sections.prose_summary: This informational text introduces yoga as a physical, mental, and spiritual practice that originated in ancient India. The word 'yoga' derives from the Sanskrit root 'yuj', meaning 'to join' or 'to unite', symbolising harmony between mind and body, thought and action, and human beings and nature. The text traces yoga's systematisation to Sage Patanjali's Yoga Sutras, which described yoga as the means to quiet the mind. The passage then outlines yoga's well-documented health benefits under several categories: physical benefits such as improved flexibility, muscle strength, endurance, and reduced body pain; mental benefits such as stress reduction, improved sleep quality, and enhanced concentration through breathing techniques and meditation; and systemic benefits including immune system support and improved heart health. The text notes yoga's global recognition, citing the World Health Organisation's launch of the WHO mYoga app in June 2021 as a free tool to promote yoga practice worldwide. It closes with an important pedagogical point: yoga asanas are not merely physical stretching — the mind must be present with the body, each stretch coordinated with breath and awareness. A Sanskrit shloka from the Hatha Yoga Pradipika is included, emphasising the primacy of asana in hatha yoga for achieving steady posture, health, and lightness of body.

reading_for_comprehension.section_name: Let us read + Let us discuss + Let us think and reflect

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Let us do these activities before we read: 1. What do you know about yoga? Discuss in pairs and share with the whole class. 2. What is 21st June known for? What do we do on this day? 3. List as many words you know related to yoga. Share them with your classmates. 4. Let us play the game of Antakshari. The first word is 'yoga'. The next word must then begin with the letter 'a'. How many new words have you learnt?

question_bank.stem: What do you know about yoga? Discuss in pairs and share with the whole class.
What is 21st June known for? What do we do on this day?
List as many words you know related to yoga.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Let us discuss — Read the passage again and fill in the blanks with words from the box. [sleep / lifestyle / health / strength / calm / stress / balance]

question_bank.stem: Yoga is a practise that not only improves physical flexibility but also improves mental 1. ____________ and leads to a healthier 2. ______________. It involves a series of asanas, which increase muscular 3. ______________. Practising yoga also leads to a better body and mind 4. ______________ and reduces body pain such as the lower back. Moreover, the focus on breathing techniques and meditation makes the mind 5. ______________, lessens 6. ______________, and improves the quality of 7. ______________. This helps to adopt a more positive lifestyle.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Let us think and reflect — Answer the following questions.

question_bank.stem: What is the aim of yoga?
Explain the idea of unity in yoga.
What does the launch of the WHO mYoga app tell us about the importance of yoga?
Describe one long-term effect that the practice of yoga can have on a person's qualities.

listening.transcript_text: Namaste everyone! I wish to share with you all that I do simple yoga exercises with my grandfather. He has been practising yoga since he was 16 years old. My grandfather is fit and strong even at the age of 65. I am sure that this is the result of many good habits. I know that eating a balanced diet regularly and making yoga a part of his life have been hundred per cent effective. When I accompany my grandfather to the park, I see so many people — young and old — doing yogic breathing exercises. I like doing these exercises. Whenever I'm a little worried about an Inter-House competition, I always remember to do some of these exercises early morning that day. It relaxes me and makes me feel focused and calm. I plan on learning some asanas from my grandfather during the summer vacations. Wish me luck!

tasks_verbatim.task_text: You will listen to a girl talk about practising yoga. As you listen, select four true statements from the sentences given in the table.

question_bank.stem: Select the FOUR true statements from the following: 1. The girl sees people at the park doing breathing exercises. 2. Her grandfather began practising yoga sixteen years ago. 3. The girl practises some easy yoga exercises with her grandfather. 4. The girl practises breathing exercises to relax. 5. Her grandfather is fit even though he has untimely meals. 6. Her grandfather's regular yoga practise has kept him strong. 7. The girl wishes to learn asanas over the weekend.
options: The girl sees people at the park doing breathing exercises.
Her grandfather began practising yoga sixteen years ago.
The girl practises some easy yoga exercises with her grandfather.
The girl practises breathing exercises to relax.
Her grandfather is fit even though he has untimely meals.
Her grandfather's regular yoga practise has kept him strong.
The girl wishes to learn asanas over the weekend.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Participate in groups of four to share your 'yoga thoughts'. Take turns to speak and listen to others when they share their thoughts.

question_bank.stem: If you have tried yoga: share — I practise yoga because ... / My favourite thing about yoga is ... / I feel ____________ after a yoga session because ...
If you have not tried yoga yet: share — I wish to practise yoga because ... / I hope that once I begin practising yoga, I ... / The kind of exercise I want to try in yoga is ____________ because ...
The one thing in yoga that I want to practise is ____________ because ...

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Write a short paragraph on 'The Benefits of Yoga'. You may use the following sentence starters along with your own ideas to create the paragraph.

question_bank.stem: Write a short paragraph on 'The Benefits of Yoga' using these starters: Opening: Yoga is important because ... | Supporting: One benefit of yoga that interests me is ... / Practising yoga can help people ... / A popular yoga pose is _____ because ... / Yoga is not just physical exercise; it also ... | Concluding: Finally, yoga can be a great way to ...

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Read the extract given below and focus on the highlighted words. The word 'practice' is a noun and 'practise' is a verb. Complete the sentences given below by filling in the blanks with correct use of practice (as a noun) or practise (as a verb). Share the answers with your classmates and the teacher.

question_bank.stem: It is important to have a regular _____________ of stretching every morning to stay flexible.
I _____________ Yoga every day to keep my mind and body healthy.
The teacher said, you can join the school hockey team but it requires a lot of _____________ to improve your skills.
Our athletics coach asked us to _____________ running on grass to strengthen our grip while running.
I _____________ deep breathing exercises when I feel stressed to calm myself down.
Developing a good habit of eating fruits and vegetables should be a daily _____________.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Another common word that has the 's' and 'c' variation in spellings is 'advice' (noun) and 'advise' (verb). Make two sentences each using 'advice' and 'advise' and share with your classmates and the teacher.

question_bank.stem: Write two sentences using 'advice' (noun).
Write two sentences using 'advise' (verb).

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Make as many words as you can using the letters shown (Y, r, e, a, n, G, o). Use the letter 'A' in all the words that you make.

question_bank.stem: Using the letters Y, r, e, a, n, G, o — make as many words as you can that all contain the letter 'A'.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Do you know? A few seals of the Sindhu-Sarasvati Civilisation, dating back to 2700 BC, indicate that yoga was prevalent in ancient India... On 11th December 2014, the United Nations General Assembly (UNGA) with 193 members accepted the proposal to celebrate '21st June' as 'International Yoga Day'. Find out about two asanas and their benefits, and share your findings with your classmates and teacher.

question_bank.stem: Find out about two yoga asanas and their benefits. Share your findings with your classmates and teacher. You may visit: https://www.mygov.in/campaigns/international-yoga-day/ or https://www.un.org/en/observances/yoga-day
